rationale_by_leg.zai-glm-5p2: Leads with the two huge math stories and correctly disaggregates them: the Jacobian counterexample (Fable 5 + Alpoge; kimmonismus 2.7k likes, tszzl, emollick) vs the separate OpenAI-model Erdos/containment-escape thread. Every granular detail traces to the snapshot (NanoGPT, PR #287, token split/obfuscation, Axios sourcing on the China-model curbs, Kirin 32.5nm). Verify tags match the evidence; counter-hypotheses are real (tool-not-autonomous, safety-pipeline-working-as-designed, distillation debunk). Main weakness: it omits the day's biggest engagement cluster entirely — the Hugging Face autonomous breach / open-vs-closed guardrails debate (DavidSacks 18k, callebtc 12k, Roemmele 5.9k). Clean format, signal-only, consistent headlines/telegram.
rationale_by_leg.claude: Builds a coherent front page around the day's dominant China/open-weight theme (HF breach -> Chinese-model curbs -> zero-Nvidia 1GW datacenter), all well-hedged, and captures Frozen v2 and the Kimi-K3 date. But it drops the Jacobian 'AI solves 87-year problem' story completely. Faithfulness slips: it labels the curbs a 'Politico report' when the dominant sourcing is Axios (A got this right), and its skeptic entry on the OpenAI escape invents '@WesRoth's elaboration' (WesRoth is absent from the snapshot) while claiming 'no OpenAI statement' despite a detailed kimmonismus tweet explicitly quoting OpenAI. Solid writing and format, but weaker source hygiene.
